name: The Withered Heath
text: You are playing Nightmare mode. Forced: After a Dragon Sign is added to the victory display, the highest [attack] enemy in the staging area engages the first player and makes an immediate attack. Forced: When the players are on stage 4B, Cold-Drake gains, “Forced: When Cold Drake attacks you, either exhaust X characters you control or give the Cold Drake +X [attack] for this attack. X is the number of Dragon signs in the victory display.”

name: The Withered Heath
text: Begin with the standard quest deck and encounter deck for The Withered Heath scenario. Remove the following cards, in the specified quantities, from the standard encounter deck: 1x Hunting the Beast (quest stage 3) 1x Lost Armory 1x Ancient Treasury 1x Cave Entrance 3x Mountain Pass 2x Dwarven Door 2x Black Bats 3x Giant Spider 2x Ruined Supplies 2x Lost in the Wild Set the Abandoned Nest and Dragon Lair locations aside. When creating the Caves deck, shuffle these two locations into it also. Then, shuffle the remaining encounter cards in this Nightmare Deck into the remainder of the standard The Withered Heath encounter deck. Finally, flip this setup card over and place it next to the quest deck. Its effects remain active throughout the game, which is now ready to begin.

name: Hunting the Beast
text: When Revealed: Shuffle 2 set-aside Dragon Sign objectives into the Caves deck. Set Cold-Drake aside, out of play. (Do not remove damage tokens from it.) Shuffle the encounter discard pile into the encounter deck and discard cards from the top until X enemies or locations are discarded, where X is the number of players. Add each discarded enemy or location to the staging area.

name: Hunting the Beast
text: Forced: At the end of the round remove three damage tokens from Cold-Drake and place 1 resource token on it. The first player exhausts X characters and looks at the top X cards of the encounter deck, reveals one of those cards and discards the rest. X is the number of resources on Cold-Drake. The players cannot defeat this stage unless at least 3 Dragon Signs are in the Victory Display. When the players advance remove all resource tokens from Cold-Drake.

name: Scion of Scatha
text: Cannot have non-objective attachments. Response: When Scion of Scatha is defeated, return it to the staging area to search the Caves deck for a Dragon Sign objective and attach it to Scion of Scatha. Shuffle the Caves deck. While guarding a card, Scion of Scatha gets +2 [attack] and adds it [threat] to the total threat in the staging area, regardless of its location.

name: Dragon Juvenile
text: When Revealed: Raise each player’s threat by X, where X is the number of Dragon Signs in the victory display.
shadow: Shadow: If this attack destroys a character, raise each player’s threat by X, where X is the number of Dragon Signs in the Victory Display.

name: Moor-stalker
text: Forced: At the beginning of the encounter phase, if Moor-stalker is in the staging area, it makes an immediate attack against the first player. If the shadow card dealt for this attack has no effect, Moor-stalker cannot be engaged this round.
shadow: Shadow: You must cancel all damage that would be dealt by this attack and exhaust a number of characters you control equal to the amount of damage canceled.

name: Abandoned Nest
text: Enemies cannot be optionally engaged, cannot attack, and cannot be attacked. Response: When Abandoned Nest is explored, raise each player’s threat by 3 to search the Caves deck for a Dragon Sign objective and reveal it.

name: Dragon Lair
text: Each player must commit at least 2 heroes to the quest each quest phase, if able. Forced: After Dragon Lair becomes the active location, reveal each Dragon Sign in the victory display. If only 1 objective was revealed this way, each player must search the encounter deck and discard pile for an enemy or location and reveal it.

name: Slopes of the Valley
text: Forced: When the players ready characters during the refresh phase, either raise each player’s threat by 2, or each player cannot ready more than 7 characters. Travel: Resolve the “when revealed” effect of the topmost treachery in the encounter discard pile.

name: Suspicious Cave
text: Each Dragon enemy gets +1 [threat], +1 [attack], +1 [defense] and cannot be optionally engaged. When Revealed: Search the encounter deck and discard pile for a Dragon enemy (2 enemies instead if there are 3 or more players) and add it to the staging area. Shuffle the encounter deck.

name: Worms of the North
text: When Revealed: Cold-Drake makes an immediate attack against the first player. Attach this card to Cold-Drake. If Cold-Drake is not in play this card gains surge. Counts as a Condition attachment with the text, “Attached enemy gets +3 hitpoints and cannot be attacked by allies. Refresh Action: Each player may exhaust one hero they control to discard Worms of the North.”

name: Frozen to the Bone
text: When Revealed: Each player must discard cards from their hand at random until they have X cards remaining, where X is the number of Dragon Sign objectives in the victory display. If any player discarded 2 or less cards, Frozen to the Bone gains surge.
shadow: Shadow: If this attack destroys a character, you must discard X attachments you control, where X is the amount of excess damage dealt by this attack.

name: Follow the Tracks
text: When Revealed: Discard cards from the encounter deck until a location is discarded and add it to the staging area. That location gets +2 (threat) and loses the Deep keyword until the end of the round. The players must travel there during the travel phase, if able. No more than three progress may be placed on the active location each round. While Follow the Tracks is in the victory display it also counts as a Dragon Sign objective in the victory display.
